author: PC Gamer
text:
Marathon's Vault Breaker PvE mode is coming in July, and here's the twist: You leave your loot behind
This is the full-on Marathon PvE experience I've been waiting for.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Amidst mounting fears of Xbox closures, Todd Howard says Arkane is 'doing a really, really good job' on Blade
Bethesda's boss confirmed Marvel's Blade is still in the works.

author: PC Gamer
text:
After upsetting everyone by threatening to ruin Diablo 4's most powerful loot in its next season, Blizzard has decided that maybe it wasn't such a good idea after all
The Season of Death Awakening looks a lot more promising now.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Grand Theft Auto 6 puts another body in the trunk: Lords of the Fallen 2 is delayed to early 2027
The delay will provide developers with more time for "iteration and polishing," and will also let CI Games avoid "a highly competitive holiday period."

author: PC Gamer
text:
EA exec says AI has helped drive 'a real rise of creativity' at its studios
Laura Miele says removing the "tedious tasks" of game development has resulted in "shorter, faster conversations around creativity and coming to alignment."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Grand Theft Auto 6 'VIP early access' scam sites are already popping up, Malwarebytes warns
Don't fall for it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Fan revolt forces RPG studio to kill its new launcher just 19 hours after release
Which you could probably have seen coming.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Italy's second-largest independent game studio buys its freedom from Embracer
34BigThings, known for games including Redout and Carmageddon: Rogue Shift, is now back in the hands of its original founders.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The grim industry summer continues as EA lays off staff ahead of $55 billion sale to Saudi Arabia, likely to soothe the sting of its $20 billion debt
The layoffs will continue until… well, something improves.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Final Fantasy 14 has done something I did not expect—it's actually fixed one of the most annoying things about its newest ultimate raid
No more arrow shenanigans… right?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Valve greenlights SteamOS installs on normal PCs with AMD GPUs, so you can go make your own Steam Machine if you don't wanna fork over $1,049
Not all of us have Machine money.

author: PC Gamer
text:
We could've had a 'very Dream Daddy-inspired' Destiny dating sim, but management said no fun
A bunch of cool game jam concepts never made it further than that.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Games Workshop never detailed how the Skitarii's most iconic weapon reloads, so Fatshark helped model it after a gaming classic
"A cool mix between classic weaponry and sci-fi that's so unique for 40k."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Best Darktide Skitarii build
Transform into a killing machine with this setup.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Blending my way through Hive Tertium's tunnels as a towering murderbot might make the Skitarii my favorite Darktide class yet
Sorry Zealot, you don't have Transonic Blades.

author: PC Gamer
text:
True to its word, AMD brings FSR 4.1 to Radeon RX 7000-series card owners with its latest Adrenalin drivers
The Steam Machine will eventually get it too. Whaddya mean, so what?

author: PC Gamer
text:
I played that $1,000 Steam game. I regret it
Imagine how bad the people who paid for it feel.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Genshin Impact creator miHoYo has released an AI companion on Steam, an eternal student cursed to never obtain her piano degree
The studio is doubling down on its investment in AI.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Warframe dev says 'no one is celebrating' Destiny 2 winding down: 'A game is healthy when you have competitors'
We all lift together.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Sand: Raiders of Sophie is like Sea of Thieves on land, but even tougher for solo players
Cruise the dunes in search of loot and get into cannon battles with other salty crews.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'Our game isn't gore porn' says director of game whose trailer features a zombie baby's skull being crushed and someone's eye getting scooped out with a knife
Ill's extreme gore-horror actually draws inspiration from Half-Life 2's Ravenholm: "We are doing our best to achieve that exact same effect, but with modern standards."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Realistic Roblox FPS made by two people hits 7 million plays, reminding us that Roblox games don't have to be terrible
TTK is a tactical shooter inspired by Ready Or Not.

author: PC Gamer
text:
An absurdly large patch filled with over 2K lines of changes is how the devs of early access co-op RPG Fellowship hope to hook players on running MMO-like dungeons without the MMO
Season 3 brings its biggest and most important update ever.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I usually skip soulslikes not made by FromSoftware, but one Steam demo just won me over
Me to my fiancee when I'm playing Prison of Husks and I reach the Prison of Husks: "That's the Prison of Husks."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Stop Killing Games says 'the fight goes on' after European Commission rejects proposed rule changes: 'We're much more than just this single petition'
The outcome is disappointing but not terribly surprising.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Steam finally found a way to recommend new games that doesn't feel like I'm getting KO'd by a fire hose
A calendar for games? Imagine!

author: PC Gamer
text:
Valve says it isn't subsidizing the Steam Machine's $1050 price because of its 'religious' refusal to 'build a more closed system'
The company says subsidizing hardware costs is ultimately worse for the consumer.

author: PC Gamer
text:
After a delay in 2025 and two delays this year (plus two more short delays this morning) mech fortress-builder Sand has finally launched
A few shaky starts, but it finally stepped over the starting line.

author: PC Gamer
text:
They're making a Sea of Thieves movie and the more I think about it, the more I'm into it
There's barrels of fun for everyone, and you'll get treasures by the ton!

author: PC Gamer
text:
Eidos Montreal founder says modern execs are driven more by spreadsheets than passion for games
Executive is short for Excel, uh, cutive.
